description_long: This sculpture consists of a large, roughly hewn stone block set atop a two-tiered stone base. The top of the block features an abstract arrangement of jagged, metallic-looking elements that protrude upward, creating a dynamic contrast with the natural stone. One side of the block displays a metal plaque, while another side is inscribed with the words 'LIMA A LIMA'. The stone has a natural, weathered appearance with visible veins and color variations, and the base is constructed from rectangular stone tiles. The overall form is monolithic and solid, suggesting permanence and weight. The sculpture is likely intended as a commemorative or artistic installation in a public space.
description: A large, rough stone block with abstract metal elements on top, set on a stepped stone base. Features a plaque and an inscription reading 'LIMA A LIMA'.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rough stone block and the abstract metal elements on top, with the stepped stone base clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side reveals a metal plaque embedded in the stone, with the metal elements on top visible from a different angle.
description_view_2: The back view highlights the natural stone texture and the stepped base, with the metal elements on top seen from behind.
description_view_3: This side displays the inscription 'LIMA A LIMA' carved into the stone, with the base and metal elements also visible.
attribute_subject_matter: abstract stone block with metal elements and commemorative inscription
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be installed in an outdoor urban or public setting, likely a plaza or park, as suggested by the stone base and commemorative plaque.
